# Copilot Instructions — Naturanza Food Website

## STRICT RULE — Dead Code Removal

When you modify, replace, or rewrite any existing code:

1. DELETE the old version completely — never leave it commented out or sitting unused.

2. If a CSS class, function, or HTML section is replaced — remove the old one entirely.

3. Never keep two versions of the same thing side by side.

4. After every edit, check for:
   - Duplicate CSS classes doing the same job
   - Old functions that are no longer called
   - Commented-out blocks of old UI or logic
   - Duplicate HTML sections (two navbars, two heroes, etc.)
   - CSS rules targeting elements that no longer exist in HTML

5. If unsure whether old code is still needed — ASK before keeping it. Default is DELETE.

6. Every file must be cleaner after your edit than before.
